title: Thuvasaur
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Update|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 13]], [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 16]]}} {{Species |image=[[File:Thuvasaur.jpg]] |name=Thuvasaur |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Non-sentient]]''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' |class=[[Reptile/Legends|Reptile]] |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Brown]], with black stripes |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor=Brown |distinctions=*A horn at the tip of the nose *3 large claws on each foot |lifespan= |origin=[[Eiattu 6]] |habitat= |diet=[[Diet/Legends|Carnivore]] |language= }} {{Quote|The stable master says that the thuvasaurs were transported to Eiattu 6 specifically for sport. Apparently they adapt quickly to harsh environments. In a matter of time they had destroyed the local predators and moved to the top of the food chain.|Wes Janson|}} '''Thuvasaurs''' were carnivorous predators imported to the planet [[Eiattu 6]]. The thuvasaur was not a native creature, but was transported to Eiattu specifically for sport. Due to their ability to adapt to harsh environments, they quickly killed off the local predator and moved to the top of the food chain. They were said to be a distant cousin of the [[Dewback/Legends|dewback]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|…says that the thuvasaur is a distant cousin to the dewback.''"
"''That so? I thought the dewback family was only native to dry worlds, like Tatooine or Breeka.|Wes Janson and Tycho Celchu|}} Thuvasaurs were a type of [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]], [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptile]] that were a distant cousin of the [[dewback]].[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 14|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 14]] They were quadrupeds with three [[Color/Legends|black]] clawed toes on each of their feet and a fourth claw on their heel. They were large enough for a [[Human/Legends|human]] to ride on their back. Thuvasaurs were predominantly brown, with darker brown scales covering the upper half of their body and outer sides of their [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], and light brown scales covering their underside including half of their thick tail and their spiked throats. Thuvasaurs had a brown [[Eye/Legends|eye]] surrounded by a semicircle of spikes on either side of their head and a single horn located at the tip of their [[Nose/Legends|nose]] above their [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]]. ==Behavior== {{Quote|The thuvasaurs went from being tame, domestic animals to cold-blooded predators.|Wes Janson|}} Thuvasaurs were at some point domesticated and tamed, but after adapting to the harsh conditions of the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Eiattu 6]] they became cold-blooded predators. Unlike their cousins in the dewback family, thuvasaurs were capable of harming others and dwelt in non-dry habitats, although they still did not like deep [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]]. Expert creature handlers could train fierce thuvasaurs to act as riding animals, strapping [[Saddle/Legends|saddles]] to their backs and [[bridle]]s in their mouths. ==History== {{Quote|I thought the stable keeper told you thuvasaurs didn't like the deeper swamps…''"
"''Maybe it's something else.|Tycho Celchu and Wes Janson|}} Thuvasaurs were imported to [[Eiattu 6]] for sport as tame and domesticated animals, but once there they adapted to the harsh environment. In [[Time/Legends|time]], they destroyed all of the local predators and moved to the top of the [[Foodstuff/Legends|food chain]]. During the [[Great Galactic War]], thuvasaurs were used as mounts or kept decoratively in some buildings and could be purchased for 50,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Galactic Strongholds]]'' In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], a stable master on Eiattu 6 spoke with the [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] about thuvasaurs. Janson later relayed the information to fellow [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] and [[Ibtisam]] while they were patrolling the wilderness of Eiattu 6 near some deep swamps. Celchu and Ibtisam were surprised that the thuvasaur and dewback were related given the differences between the two creatures. When the pilot [[Dllr Nep]] heard something in the swamps, Antilles and Celchu realized it was probably not thuvasaurs in that environment, so went to investigate and discovered [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|forces]]. ==Behind the scenes== The thuvasaur was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 14|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 14]], which was written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and [[Scott Tolson]] and published on [[December 1]], [[1996]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/96-487/Star-Wars-X-Wing-Rogue-Squadron-14The-Warrior-Princess-Part-2-of-4|text=Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron #14 The Warrior Princess (Part 2 of 4)}} The species was first depicted in the [[Star Wars video games|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', where they became available as part of Game Update 5.2.2 on [[June 13]], [[2017]]. Initially only available through [[Grand Chance Cube]]s bought in the [[Cartel Market]], they were then one of the items included in the "Dread Warlord Pack" on [[June 27]] and eventually became individually purchasable for 1,500 Cartel Coins, a premium in-game currency. ==Appearances== *[[Cartel Market|''Star Wars: The Old Republic'' Cartel Market]] {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Galactic Strongholds]]'' *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 13|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 13]] {{1stm}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 14|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 14]] {{Mo}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 16|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 16]] {{Mo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Thuvasaurier }} [[Category:Non-sentient species]] [[Category:Predatory creatures]] [[Category:Reptiles]]
